description: 1944 landing operations of the Allied invasion of Normandy
description_wiki: The Normandy landings were the landing operations and associated airborne operations on 6 June 1944 of the Allied invasion of Normandy in Operation Overlord during the Second World War.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description_wiki: Fort Riley is a United States Army installation located in North Central Kansas, on the Kansas River, also known as the Kaw, between Junction City and Manhattan.

description: air service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Air Force (USAF) is the air service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description_wiki: Richard T. Crane Medical Prep High School (formerly known as Crane Tech Prep or Crane Tech High School) is a public four-year medical prep high school located in the Near West Side neighborhood of Chicago, Illinois, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Salt Lake County, Utah, United States and the capital of Utah
description_wiki: Salt Lake City, often shortened to Salt Lake or SLC, is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Utah.

description_wiki: Boise (locally   also ) is the capital and most populous city in the U.S. state of Idaho.

description: American football player and coach (1915-1985)
description_wiki: Cecil Frank Isbell (July 11, 1915 – June 23, 1985) was an American professional football quarterback  and coach.

description: capital of the state of Kansas, United States; county seat of Shawnee County
description_wiki: Topeka ( tə-PEE-kə) is the capital city of the U.S. state of Kansas and the county seat of Shawnee County.

description: city in and county seat of Onondaga County, New York, United States
description_wiki: Syracuse ( SIRR-ə-kewz, SERR-, -⁠kewss) is a city in New York, United States, and the county seat of Onondaga County.

description: city in Maine, United States
description_wiki: Presque Isle ( PRESK) is the commercial center and largest city in Aroostook County, Maine, United States.

description: town in province of Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
description_wiki: Gander is a town located in the northeastern part of the island of Newfoundland in the Canadian province of Newfoundland and Labrador, approximately  40 km (25 mi) south of Gander Bay,  100 km (62 mi) south of Twillingate and  90 km (56 mi) east of Grand Falls-Windsor.

description: town in South Ayrshire, Scotland, UK
description_wiki: Prestwick (Scottish Gaelic: Preastabhaig) is a town in South Ayrshire on the west coast of Ayrshire in Scotland about 30 miles (50 kilometres) southwest of Glasgow.

description: market town and civil parish in the Borough of Dacorum, Hertfordshire, England, UK
description_wiki: Tring  is a market town and civil parish in the Borough of Dacorum, in Hertfordshire, England.

description: capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom
description_wiki: London is the capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom, with a population of 9.1 million people in 2024.

description: 1939 fighter aircraft family by Focke-Wulf
description_wiki: The Focke-Wulf Fw 190, nicknamed Würger (lit. 'Shrike') is a German single-seat, single-engined, fighter aircraft designed by Kurt Tank at Focke-Wulf in the late 1930s and widely used during World War II. Along with its well-known counterpart, the Messerschmitt Bf 109, the Fw 190 became the backbone of the Jagdwaffe (Fighter Force) of the Luftwaffe.

description: numbered air force (NAF) of the United States Air Force's Air Force Global Strike Command (AFGSC)
description_wiki: The Eighth Air Force (Air Forces Strategic) is a numbered air force (NAF) of the United States Air Force's Air Force Global Strike Command (AFGSC).

description: United States Air Force wing, part of the Kansas Air National Guard
world's first short-range guided ballistic missile
description_wiki: The V-2 rocket (German: Vergeltungswaffe 2, lit. 'Vengeance Weapon 2'), with the development name Aggregat-4 (A4), was the world's first practical, modern ballistic missile.

description: American military officer and aviation pioneer (1896–1993)

entity: Surgeon General of the United States
aliases: Office of the Surgeon General of the United States
description: head of the U.S. Public Health Service Commissioned Corps
description_wiki: The surgeon general of the United States is the operational head of the United States Public Health Service Commissioned Corps (PHSCC) and thus a leading spokesperson on matters of public health in the federal government of the United States.
